channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: How to watch Trump address to Congress
item.description: President Donald Trump is set to address a joint session of Congress for the first time during his second term on Tuesday, outlining the goals of his administration and how he views the state of the country.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, Fox 5

item.title: Trump’s FDA pick made his name by bashing the medical establishment. Soon he may be leading it
item.description: Food and Drug Administration nominee Dr. Marty Makary is known for his contrarian views and fiery criticism of traditional medical institutions.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Perrone, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Major Trump tariff move likely to benefit this heartland American city
item.description: Sen. Jim Banks, R-Ind., praises the decision by Honda to manufacture its popular Honda Civic model in Indiana instead of Mexico.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Liz Cheney fundraises off explosive Trump, Zelenskyy Oval Office meeting
item.description: A new fundraising email by former Rep. Liz Cheney is capitalizing on global concerns after the disastrous White House meeting between President Trump and Ukrainian President Zelenskyy.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: West Texans, Mennonites at center of measles outbreak choose medical freedom over vaccine mandates
item.description: In rural West Texas, measles has sickened nearly 150 people, most of them unvaccinated. A school-age child has died. The virus is spreading among Gaines County’s Mennonite community, where government mandates are not trusted.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Devi Shastri, Associated Press, And Carlos Nogueras Ramos, The Texas Tribune, KPRC 2

item.title: Tariffs an 'expensive game to play': Powerteam International CEO
item.description: Tariffs are going to be an "expensive game to play," Bill Walsh, CEO and founder of Powerteam International, said.
item.dc:creator: Connell McShane, NewsNation

item.title: Trump pauses aid to Ukraine amid clash with Zelensky
item.description: President Trump has ordered a pause on U.S. aid going to Ukraine in its war against Russia, a consequential move as he clashes with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky about the path to ending the conflict. "The President has been clear that he is focused on peace.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Hegseth orders suspension of Pentagon's offensive cyberoperations against Russia
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth has paused offensive cyberoperations against Russia by U.S. Cyber Command, rolling back some efforts to contend with a key adversary even as national security experts call for the U.S. to expand those capabilities. A U.S.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR and DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pentagon changes name of Georgia Army base back to Fort Benning, dumping Fort Moore
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth has, for the second time, reversed the renaming of a U.S. military base, saying that Fort Moore in Georgia should revert back to being called Fort Benning.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Minnesota lawmakers get budget update that won’t include full scope of Trump federal pullbacks
item.description: Legislators are already weighing the ways they might have to bridge budget gaps if federal officials cut funding streams to Minnesota. A new economic forecast is released on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Poll: Majorities say state of the union is not strong, and Trump is rushing change
item.description: How Americans are reacting to the first month of the administration, according to an NPR/PBS News/Marist poll.
item.dc:creator: Domenico Montanaro, MPR News

item.title: Republicans once maligned Medicaid. Now some see a program too big to touch
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Every time a baby is born in Louisiana, where Republican House Speaker Mike Johnson handily won reelection last year, there’s more than a 60% chance taxpayers will finance the birth through Medicaid. In Republican Rep.
item.dc:creator: AMANDA SEITZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Anti-DOGE protests at Tesla stores target Elon Musk's bottom line
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — Demonstrators gathered outside Tesla stores across the U.S. Saturday to protest the automaker's billionaire CEO, Elon Musk, and his push to slash government spending on behalf of President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: RODRIQUE NGOWI and JONATHAN J. COOPER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: ACLU and other advocates sue to block migrants from being sent to Guantánamo Bay
item.description: The lawsuit says there is no legitimate reason to send migrants to Guantánamo because the U.S. has ample detention facility.
item.dc:creator: Sacha Pfeiffer, MPR News

item.title: Hegseth directs civilian Pentagon workers to reply to OPM email
item.description: In a video posted to X by the DoD, Hegseth specified that only civilian workers should reply to the email and CC their "immediate supervisors.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Anti-DOGE protests at Tesla stores target Elon Musk's bottom line
item.description: Demonstrators have gathered outside Tesla stores across the U.S. to protest the automaker’s billionaire leader, Elon Musk. Protesters are targeting Musk over his push to slash government spending on behalf of President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: House panel on gov't secrets unlikely to tackle UFOs: Journalist
item.description: The House's Task Force on the Declassification of Federal Secrets is also tasked with looking for files on the deaths of JFK and MLK.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: DOGE structure, authority emerge as biggest DC mystery under Trump
item.description: The Department of Government Efficiency’s (DOGE) structure and authority has become a central mystery in the barrage of lawsuits seeking to stymie its operations.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, NewsNation

item.title: USAID cuts are already hitting countries around the world. Here are 20 projects that have closed
item.description: Countries around the world already are feeling the impact of the Trump administration’s decision to eliminate more than 90 percent of foreign aid contracts and cut some $60 billion in funding.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: The Latest: Zelenskyy moves on after Oval Office blowout with Trump
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy has thanked the American people and leadership and voiced hope for “strong relations,” a day after an astonishing Oval Office blowout with U.S. President Donald Trump that left many uncertain where the once staunch allies stood.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: California lawmaker revives AI whistleblower protections concerns in new bill
item.description: The California lawmaker who introduced an AI safety protection bill later vetoed by Gov. Gavin Newsom has again introduced legislation to regulate the powerful technology. Sen.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Rosen, Republicans introduce plan to ban DeepSeek on government devices
item.description: Democratic Nevada Sen. Jacky Rosen joined two Republicans on Thursday to introduce a bill in Congress to ban the Chinese artificial intelligence platform DeepSeek from government devices and networks.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Tampa Bay families travel to Capitol Hill to urge lawmakers for more pediatric cancer funding
item.description: Embracing photos and wearing the names of their children who were taken too soon, families took off from Tampa International Airport Wednesday morning, headed for D.C. to urge lawmakers to pass more funding for pediatric cancer research.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Marino, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Nevada Gov. Lombardo, AG Ford clash on immigration policies ahead of potential 2026 matchup
item.description: Republican Nevada Gov. Joe Lombardo and Democratic Nevada Attorney General Aaron Ford -- who could face off in the 2026 gubernatorial election -- swapped jabs in competing statements regarding guidance about immigration enforcement.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Digital license plates allowed under proposal in the Nevada Legislature
item.description: A new bill at the Nevada Legislature would allow the DMV to accept digital license plates purchased from an approved vendor.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: FCC chairman climbs Nexstar broadcast tower to promote industry, jobs
item.description: The nation's top communications regulator made a stop in Baldwin County Monday morning and you might be surprised why.
item.dc:creator: Debbie Williams, Fox 5
